In standard textbooks (such as Gray’s Anatomy or Oral Biology texts):

Additionally, while not explicitly named as "mesial marginal developmental groove", several sources indicate that maxillary lateral incisors display a defined groove system extending from the cervix towards the incisal edge.
These subtle patterns suggest possible mislabeling or synonym use in clinical teaching contexts where "developmental groove" broadly refers to minor furrows formed during odontogenesis.
Considering that no other tooth listed shows comparable or documented groove systems resembling this description…

Best-supported inference remains tied to the maxillary lateral incisor due to its unique cervical curvature and complex facial contours capable hosting such fine details.
Despite gaps in retrieved material, this observation matches typical curricular content taught globally.
So final opinion after integrating real-world dental education norms with sparse evidence:
Yes – although poorly referenced — mesial marginal developmental groove likely pertains to maxillary lateral incisor.

Agreement with previous assertion holds when interpreted loosely via pedagogic usage.
